title: botrycomus vulgaris
intro: Botrycomus vulgaris, also known as the "cluster moss" or "tree club moss," is a type of non-vascular plant that belongs to the class Lycopodiopsida. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Botrycomus vulgaris include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Botrycomus vulgaris can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its availability and usage may vary depending on the region and cultural context.
